title: Add saved-search and notification on new matches
business_context: Users repeatedly search for same criteria. Add saved searches with notification when new results appear.

files.content: export const search = async (req: any, res: any) => { /* one-off */ };

reasoning: SavedSearch model with criteria. Worker every 5 min reruns each query; compares with lastRun.matchIds; emits notifications for new matches.

after_state.description: SavedSearch + worker + notification on new match.

files.purpose: Periodic check + notify on new matches

testing_strategy: New match: insert matching article, worker notifies. Dedup: same matches twice yield one notification.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would notify on every match - spam. Diffing against lastMatchIds avoids it.

title: Add query-result hashing to detect cache key collisions
business_context: Cache keys generated from query params; bug caused collisions returning wrong data. Need hashing strategy that's collision-resistant.

files.content: export function cacheKey(prefix: string, params: Record<string, unknown>): string { return `${prefix}:${JSON.stringify(params)}`; }

reasoning: Canonicalize params (sorted keys, normalized values) then SHA256 hash. Short collision-resistant key.
testing_strategy: Collision test: keys for {a:1,b:2} and {b:2,a:1} equal. SHA strength: no realistic collision risk.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would use Object.keys order - depends on insertion order, breaks dedup.

title: Add live signature/contract page with cursor visualization and multi-party signing flow
business_context: E-signature page lets two parties sign in real-time. Each sees the other's cursor and signature live.

files.content:
export function Sign() { return
Sign here
; }

reasoning: Signature room. Each party emits cursor + final signature stroke. Server stores signature paths on commit. Audit each event.

after_state.description: Two-party live signature room + audit on commit.

migration_strategy: Step 4: PDF export of signed doc
testing_strategy: Two clients: A signs, both see status update. Cursor visible. Audit entry created on each sign.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would skip audit - regulatory issue for e-signatures.

title: Add 3D maze game with first-person controls and collision detection
business_context: Educational game needs first-person maze navigation. Need WASD controls + collision detection against walls.

files.content:
export function Maze() { return
Game placeholder
; }

reasoning: PointerLockControls for mouse look. WASD updates camera position with speed*delta. Collision via AABB against wall meshes. Disposes on unmount.
testing_strategy: Move test: WASD moves. Collision: cannot pass walls. Mouse look: yaw + pitch.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would skip collisions or use raycaster per frame for every wall - costly. AABB is fast.

title: Add tenant-aware webhook test endpoint for customer integration debugging
business_context: Customers debugging their webhook handler need a way to fire test events. Need test endpoint per tenant generating sample events.

files.content: // no test

reasoning: POST /webhooks/:id/test sends a sample event payload to the subscriber, including a header x-event-test=true. Audit each test.

after_state.description: Test endpoint sends sample event with test header.

files.purpose: Worker forwards test header if present

testing_strategy: Test fires: receiver sees x-event-test header. Sample payload shape matches production. Audit recorded.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would use real production events - confuses billing.

title: Add nonce-based replay protection on idempotent POST endpoints
business_context: Mobile clients sometimes retry POSTs after timeout, causing duplicate orders. Add Idempotency-Key support similar to Stripe.

files.content: export const create = async (req: any, res: any) => { /* creates order */ };

reasoning: Idempotency-Key header. Server checks (userId, key) -> cached response. If present return cached. If absent, run handler and cache response.
testing_strategy: Retry test: same key returns cached. New key creates new. TTL: after 24h, new attempt creates new.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would dedupe by request body hash - if body changes slightly (timestamp) miss.

title: Refactor optimistic mutations across app to reusable mutation-with-rollback pattern
business_context: Optimistic updates scattered across hooks - each ad-hoc. Adopt a unified mutation+rollback helper.

files.content:
import { useState } from 'react';
export function useLike(initial: number) { const [n, setN] = useState(initial); return { n, like: async () => { setN(n + 1); try { await fetch('/like', { method: 'POST' }); } catch { setN(n); } } }; }

reasoning: Use TanStack Query useMutation with onMutate/onError pattern. Provide useOptimistic wrapper.
testing_strategy: Success: cache updated. Error: rollback restores. Concurrent: cancel previous fetch.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would forget to cancel in-flight - races between optimistic and refetch.

title: Adopt structured cookies via Express signed cookies and unified secret management
business_context: Cookies set ad-hoc across controllers. Adopt signed cookies and centralized config.

files.content: export const login = async (req: any, res: any) => { res.cookie('user', 'u1'); res.json({ ok: true }); };

reasoning: Centralize via setSafeCookie helper that applies HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Lax, signs by default. Reads via req.signedCookies.
migration_strategy: Step 3: Lint rule against res.cookie directly
testing_strategy: Cookie attrs: HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Lax, signed. Read: req.signedCookies.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would skip signing - vulnerable to tampering.

title: Add report-builder with drag-drop fields and live preview
business_context: Users want to build custom reports without code. Drag fields onto canvas, configure aggregation, see live preview.

files.content:
export function Reports() { return
Static report
; }

reasoning: FieldRegistry exposes available fields with types. UI drag-drop into selection. Backend translates definition into aggregation pipeline.

after_state.description: Field registry + drag-drop UI + safe pipeline builder.

testing_strategy: Definition validation: invalid field rejected. Pipeline executes. Result shape consistent.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would let users specify raw aggregation pipeline - injection risk.

title: Reduce Mongo connection pool churn by avoiding per-request connections
business_context: Each request creates short-lived Mongoose model instances causing connection churn. Configure pool properly and reuse connections.

files.content:
import mongoose from 'mongoose';
export async function connect() { await mongoose.connect(process.env.MONGO_URL!); }
issues: Default pool too small under load

reasoning: Configure maxPoolSize + minPoolSize. Add health check + graceful shutdown closing connections.
testing_strategy: Concurrent test: 500 RPS, pool saturates at 50 without errors. Shutdown: SIGTERM closes connections.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would create new Mongoose connections per request - fd exhaustion.

title: Add live polling app with vote count animation and result reveal
business_context: Audience polls during a presentation. Live vote counts shown to admin. Reveal results to audience on cue.

files.content:
export function Poll() { return
Poll
; }

reasoning: Poll model with options. Vote stored uniquely per (pollId, voterToken). Server emits counts to admin always; to audience only if revealed.

after_state.description: Per-voter dedup + admin-only counts until reveal.

files.purpose: Audience hidden counts until reveal; admin always sees

migration_strategy: Step 3: Vote dedup via unique index
testing_strategy: Vote-once: second vote ignored. Reveal: audience receives counts. Without reveal: audience sees question only.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would send counts to everyone - audience sees votes before reveal.

title: Add 3D bookshelf with click-to-open book detail
business_context: Digital library uses 3D bookshelf. Click a book pulls it out and shows details.

files.content:
export function Books({ items }: { items: { title: string }[] }) { return
    {items.map((b, i) =>
  • {b.title}
    • )}

testing_strategy: Click: book moves outward. Detail panel updates.

title: Add tenant-scoped feature changelog visible to tenant admins
business_context: Tenants want to see what changed in their plan-tier features. Need per-tier changelog visible to tenant admins.

files.content: // none

reasoning: ReleaseNote model with tiers array. Tenant sees notes whose tiers include theirs. Read tracking via UserReadState.

files.purpose: List with read state per user

testing_strategy: Tier filter: free user doesn't see pro-only notes. Read state per user.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would show all notes to all tiers - confusion.

title: Add print-friendly export with semantic HTML and CSS print stylesheet
business_context: Reports need printable output. Need clean HTML with @media print rules - no JS, no nav, page breaks correct.

files.content:
export function Report() { return
Report content
; }

reasoning: Add @media print CSS hiding nav, sizing pages, controlling breaks. Add print-friendly route generating semantic HTML.

files.purpose: Semantic report with page breaks per section

testing_strategy: Print preview: nav hidden, sections paginate. PDF: looks correct.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would use absolute positioning - breaks print layout.

title: Add audit-log integrity via hash chaining to detect tampering
business_context: Audit logs can be modified by anyone with DB access. Hash-chain logs so tampering is detectable.
reasoning: Each log includes prevHash and selfHash = SHA256(prevHash + canonicalBody). Verifier walks chain. Periodic anchor (latest selfHash) written to immutable storage (S3 object-lock or signed external).
migration_strategy: Step 4: Anchor latest hash to S3 with object lock
testing_strategy: Tamper test: modify body, verify fails. Verifier returns first bad id.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would skip canonicalization - hash differs based on key order.

title: Refactor pagination state into URL + cache + prefetch pattern
business_context: Paginated list rebuilds state on navigation. Move pagination to URL with cache + prefetch next page.

files.content:
import { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
export function List() { const [page, setPage] = useState(0); const [items, setItems] = useState<any[]>([]); useEffect(() => { fetch(`/api/list?page=${page}`).then(r => r.json()).then(setItems); }, [page]); return <button onClick={() => setPage(p => p + 1)}>Next</button>; }

reasoning: useSearchParams for page. useQuery keyed on page. Prefetch page+1 via queryClient.prefetchQuery on idle.
migration_strategy: Step 1: Migrate page state to URL
testing_strategy: Deep link: visit ?page=3, loads page 3. Prefetch: next page in cache when clicking Next.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would prefetch immediately - costs bandwidth. Idle callback throttles.

title: Add ETag-aware HTTP/2 server push for critical CSS to cut FCP
business_context: FCP delayed by separate CSS request. Use 103 Early Hints + preload hint via Link header to start CSS download early.

files.content: // no link header

reasoning: Send Link: </styles.css>; rel=preload header before main body. Modern browsers/proxies use it. CDN forwards to client.

after_state.description: Link preload header for critical CSS.

testing_strategy: FCP test: drop in measurable amount. Preload visible in network panel.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would <link rel=preload> in body - misses head priority.

title: Add presence-aware document locking - last user editing locks for grace period
business_context: Two-step approval workflow. While reviewer A is editing, prevent reviewer B from editing for 60s. Auto-release.

files.content: // none

reasoning: On focus, request lock via socket. If held by another, deny. Server stores in Redis with TTL 60s; refresh on activity. Release on blur.

after_state.description: Doc lock with TTL + admin override.

migration_strategy: Step 2: UI requests lock on focus
testing_strategy: Concurrent test: two clients lock attempts; one succeeds. TTL: lock auto-releases.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would hold lock until explicit release - browser crash leaves stuck lock.

title: Migrate monolithic Express app to fastify for higher throughput
business_context: Express is 3-5x slower than fastify. Migrate high-traffic endpoints first while keeping admin/internal on Express.
reasoning: Stand up Fastify server alongside Express. Migrate hot endpoints to Fastify. Share business logic. Reverse proxy splits traffic.

after_state.description: Fastify for hot path; Express for legacy.

files.purpose: Edge proxy routes hot endpoints to Fastify

testing_strategy: Throughput: hot endpoint 2-3x RPS. Behavior parity per endpoint.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would migrate all in one PR - risk too high.

title: Add 3D anatomical model with layered visibility toggles
business_context: Medical education needs an anatomy viewer. Layers: skeleton, muscles, organs, skin. Toggle visibility independently.
reasoning: GLB groups named by layer. Traverse to set material.opacity for each group. Lerp opacity in useFrame.
testing_strategy: Slider: opacity smooth transitions. Hidden when 0.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would set visible directly - hard cut.

title: Add SSO group → role mapping per tenant
business_context: Customers use Okta groups; mapping to our roles via admin config. Need flexible mapping per tenant.
constraints: Multiple groups can map to role

files.content: export function syncUser() { /* assigns no role */ }

reasoning: GroupRoleMapping per orgId. On SSO login, look up roles from user's groups. Cache. Audit changes.

files.purpose: Computes role from groups and audits changes

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add model + mapping admin UI
testing_strategy: User with engineers group gets engineer role. Removing group revokes. Audit captures change.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would hardcode group → role - inflexible across tenants.

title: Add request-coalescing on backend to dedupe simultaneous expensive requests
business_context: Many users hit slow endpoint simultaneously. Cache miss results in N parallel expensive computations. Coalesce in-flight requests.

files.content: export async function expensiveReport(key: string) { /* 2s compute */ return { key }; }

reasoning: Map<key, Promise<result>>. First request kicks off; subsequent share the promise. Cleanup on settle.

files.content:
import { coalesce } from '../lib/coalesce';
export async function expensiveReport(key: string) { return coalesce(`report:${key}`, async () => { /* 2s compute */ return { key }; }); }

testing_strategy: 100 parallel same-key requests - assert one compute. Different keys not blocked.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would use a TTL cache - first request still computes alone but second N-1 might still race in cache-warm window.

title: Add A/B testing framework with assignment + telemetry + statistical guards
business_context: Product wants A/B tests with deterministic assignment, telemetry collection, and basic stats. Build minimal framework.
constraints: Per-experiment guard against contaminating overlapping tests

files.content: // none

reasoning: Experiment registry with variants and target metric. Assignment = hash(userId+experimentId) mod 100 < % for variant. Server emits exposure events. Conversion tracked separately. Basic z-test for proportions.

after_state.description: Hash assignment + exposure/conversion tracking + basic z-test.

testing_strategy: Deterministic: same user repeatedly gets same variant. Reporter: known data produces expected z-value.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would use Math.random for assignment - non-sticky, contaminates data.

title: Block suspicious user-agent and ASN-based abuse via Cloudflare rules + server fallback
business_context: Bot traffic from datacenter ASNs causes scraping abuse. Block at edge (Cloudflare) with server-side fallback using ipgeolocation.

files.content: // none

reasoning: On request, lookup ASN via IP db (cached). Block if ASN in blocklist and not in known-good list. Log blocks. Forward to Cloudflare rules as primary; server is fallback.

files.purpose: ASN lookup with cache + block + audit

migration_strategy: Step 4: Push rules to Cloudflare too
testing_strategy: Blocked ASN: 403. Allowed: passes. Cache: lookup once.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would block by IP only - hot IP changes constantly. ASN is stable.

title: Adopt finite-state-machine pattern for checkout flow
business_context: Checkout has 6 states with complex transitions (cart, address, payment, review, processing, complete). Bugs from invalid transitions. Adopt XState-like pattern.

files.content: // many useState + booleans

reasoning: Small typed FSM helper. States, events, transitions, entry actions. Hook returns send + state.
testing_strategy: Invalid transition ignored. Resume: persist + rehydrate works.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A junior would booleans-per-step - lets impossible states slip through.
